product_owner_decisions_applied.name: RETAIN_DISABLED_BY_DEFAULT
product_owner_decisions_applied.description: Advanced non-default capabilities are retained in the Magna capability model but disabled by default. May be enabled by explicit Product Owner or user action from Magna UI, subject to Magna permissions, policy gates, audit, and TRACE.

product_owner_decisions_applied.name: Verdict and next-action output split
product_owner_decisions_applied.description: Chat/Telegram: short summary only, references TRACE/evidence ID. GitHub: complete verdict, evidence, next-action suggestions — canonical durable record.

product_owner_decisions_applied.name: Strong internal TRACE
product_owner_decisions_applied.description: TRACE enforced inside Magna (live internal state) and GitHub (durable). Every action must create/update TRACE event before proceeding. No dispatch, write, verdict, or closure without active TRACE envelope.

product_owner_decisions_applied.name: Epic 1 local-first primary flow
product_owner_decisions_applied.description: 13-step local Magna-controlled orchestration is the primary flow. Telegram continuation is activation-gated.

files_changed.description: Added HERMES-US-001 entry (fixes PR #35 governance CI) and HERMES-STORIES-001 entry (this correction branch)

corrections: Sprint 1 and Sprint 4 expected outcomes updated
TRACE-linked result state in user flow
Result States section added (COMPLETED, FAILED, PAUSED_FOR_APPROVAL, BLOCKED, NEEDS_REVIEW, TIMED_OUT)
Changed files vs. claimed changes separated
No continuation without valid result state in TRACE
Evidence generated from/linked to TRACE envelope
Includes detailed verdict and next-action suggestions
GitHub is canonical durable record — does not replace Magna TRACE
TRACE/evidence ID reference required in notification
Does not replace GitHub durable verdict record
User can review full verdict in GitHub before deciding
User story rewritten: inside Magna itself and in GitHub
TRACE Event Requirement section: 17 required events
Blocking requirement: no action without TRACE envelope
User story rewritten: local Magna-controlled orchestration
Magna review, verify, verdict, next-actions steps added
Short chat verdict summary + complete GitHub verdict/next-actions

pr_35_governance_ci.cause: product/hermes-runtime-adoption-stories branch had no registered task in ACTIVE_WORK_REGISTRY.yaml
pr_35_governance_ci.fix: Added HERMES-US-001 entry to registry on this correction branch
pr_35_governance_ci.merge_order_note: Product Owner must decide whether to merge this correction branch to main first (providing registry entry on main) or apply registry entry directly to the product branch

retain_disabled_by_default_capabilities: Telegram-triggered remote execution (intake-only until authorization gates are met)
telegram_authorization_gates: Messaging gateway re-authorized by Product Owner
epic_1_primary_flow: 3. Magna understands and routes the instruction.
4. Magna checks for a known instruction match.
6. Magna calls Claude or Codex according to approved worker policy.
7. Claude or Codex performs the task.
8. Worker output and evidence are written or proposed in GitHub.
9. Magna reviews worker changes and evidence.
11. Magna provides a short verdict summary in chat (later, activation-gated, also in Telegram).
12. Magna writes or proposes the complete verdict and next-action suggestions in GitHub.
13. Magna summarizes recommended next actions and waits for user approval, redirection, or stop.
trace_rule: If it happened in Magna, it must be traceable in Magna. If it matters for project history, it must also be durable in GitHub.
trace_required_events: Known instruction matched or not matched
Changed files detected (verified, not only claimed)
User approval, redirection, or stop captured

open_questions.question: Which sender boundary mechanism is approved for Telegram?
What is the first approved demonstration scenario for end-to-end flow?
Task continuation from both remote channel and ChatGPT, or one first?
Minimum TRACE envelope view required for Product Owner review?
Forward/backward traceability in Command Center UI, GitHub evidence, or both?
Should RETAIN_DISABLED_BY_DEFAULT capabilities be visible in Command Center UI when disabled?
PR #33 status — branding dependency for story 001

forward: Sprint planning (after Product Owner approval only)
